differences.title: 1. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Bun utilizes JavaScriptCore for significantly faster startup and execution.
differences.rightDesc: Node.js offers reliable and optimized performance through V8.

differences.title: 2. TOOLING
differences.leftDesc: Bun includes bundler, transpiler, test runner, and package manager.
differences.rightDesc: Node.js relies on separate tools like Webpack, Babel, Jest.

differences.title: 3. COMPATIBILITY
differences.leftDesc: Bun supports ES Modules and TypeScript out-of-the-box.
differences.rightDesc: Node.js has stronger legacy support for CommonJS.

differences.title: 4. PACKAGE MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: Bun's package manager is built-in and extremely fast.
differences.rightDesc: NPM/Yarn are widely used but can be slower.

differences.title: 5. API
differences.leftDesc: Bun implements many Web API standards for browser compatibility.
differences.rightDesc: Node.js has its own set of global objects.

differences.title: 6. ECOSYSTEM MATURITY
differences.leftDesc: Bun is newer, with a smaller but fast-expanding community.
differences.rightDesc: Node.js has a huge, mature, and stable ecosystem.

differences.title: 7. MEMORY USAGE
differences.leftDesc: Bun's JavaScriptCore engine is generally more memory-efficient.
differences.rightDesc: Node.js's V8 engine can consume more memory.
